page: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the c7a.8xlarge in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng and Geekbench testing. The processor's instruction set support, including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, contributes to strong results in extended instructions and physics simulations. Memory performance is characterized by strong write bandwidth for large blocks and high read bandwidth for cached small blocks, though uncached large-block reads fall into the average tier.

This instance demonstrates top-tier performance in small-model LLM prompt processing and strong capabilities in medium-model text generation. It is highly capable for software compilation, database operations, and Redis SET operations, where it achieves 72.17 million operations per second. Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and dedicated core allocation, the c7a.8xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads such as batch processing, scientific modeling, and distributed analytics, where raw CPU throughput is prioritized over massive memory footprints.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong performance, ranking in the top 25% for single-core and multi-core benchmarks, with notable strengths in software compilation, database operations, and Redis execution. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16, making it highly effective for compute-intensive tasks. The c7a.8xlarge is best suited for workloads requiring high CPU density and fast memory throughput, such as batch processing, web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced vector instruction support for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz.
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core).
Strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU and database benchmarks.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16.
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, software compilation, and Redis operations.
